Introduction

Learning Philosophy & My Journey

My journey into Electrical Engineering began with a simple fascination — how does electricity power the world around us? From the flicker of a light bulb to the quiet hum of a motor, I found wonder in every circuit. At PDEU, I've channelled that curiosity into a disciplined academic path that bridges theory with hands-on practice.

I believe that engineering is not just a profession — it is a responsibility. Whether it's designing smarter power systems, exploring the potential of electric vehicles, or integrating AI into industrial automation, I approach every challenge with a growth mindset and genuine passion. My internship at Reliance Industries (CPP Jamnagar) showed me that real-world problems demand creative, ethical, and collaborative solutions.

Outside the classroom, I invest time in self-growth: reading about emerging technologies, contributing to team projects, and reflecting on how engineers can build a more sustainable, equitable world. I lead with empathy, learn with consistency, and innovate with purpose.

Discipline Growth Mindset Sustainability Innovation Teamwork Ethical Engineering

Research & Innovation

Interdisciplinary Projects

Bridging core electrical engineering with artificial intelligence, IoT, and sustainable energy solutions.

⚙️

AI + Electrical Engineering

Fault Detection in Induction Motors using ANN

Objective: Develop an AI model to identify bearing faults and stator winding failures in induction motors before they cause costly breakdowns.

Methodology: Trained an Artificial Neural Network on vibration, current, and thermal signal data collected from simulated fault conditions in MATLAB. The ANN classified fault types with over 94% accuracy, enabling predictive maintenance.

Innovation: Combined signal processing with machine learning, replacing costly manual inspection with automated, real-time detection — a step toward smart industrial systems.

MATLAB ANN Signal Processing Predictive Maintenance
📡

IoT + Energy Management

Smart Energy Monitoring System using IoT

Objective: Design a real-time energy monitoring dashboard for residential/industrial use that tracks consumption and alerts users about inefficiencies.

Methodology: Used Arduino with current and voltage sensors (ACS712, ZMPT101B) to capture live power data, transmitted to a cloud dashboard (ThingSpeak) via Wi-Fi module (ESP8266). Alerts trigger when usage crosses thresholds.

Innovation: Affordable, scalable, and deployable without replacing existing meters — empowering consumers with energy intelligence.

Arduino ESP8266 ThingSpeak IoT
🔋

AI + EV + Data Analysis

EV Battery Health Prediction Model

Objective: Predict State of Health (SoH) and remaining useful life (RUL) of EV lithium-ion battery packs using machine learning.

Methodology: Processed NASA battery dataset using Python (Pandas, Scikit-learn). Applied regression and LSTM neural networks to model battery degradation patterns. Achieved prediction accuracy within ±2% SoH margin.

Innovation: Enables OEMs and EV owners to plan maintenance proactively, extending battery life and reducing replacement costs.

Python LSTM Scikit-learn EV Technology

Collaboration

Leadership & Teamwork

Engineering is a team sport. Here is how I've contributed, led, and grown through collaborative experiences.

🎯 Led Project Team – Smart Energy Monitoring System

Served as team lead for a 4-member IoT project group. Organized weekly sprint meetings, divided responsibilities based on individual strengths, and ensured deliverables were met on time. Successfully presented the working prototype to faculty evaluators, earning commendation for clarity of explanation and system robustness.

🎙️ Technical Seminar Presenter – Power Systems Symposium

Delivered a structured seminar on "Smart Grid Technologies and the Future of Energy Distribution" to an audience of peers and faculty. Developed presentation slides, rehearsed for clarity, and fielded technical questions — strengthening both domain knowledge and public speaking confidence.

🤝 Conflict Resolution & Consensus Building

During a group assignment, a technical disagreement arose over circuit design methodology. I facilitated a structured discussion, encouraged all team members to present their approaches, and guided the group toward a hybrid solution incorporating the best aspects of each. The experience taught me that productive disagreement leads to better outcomes.

📚 Peer Mentoring & Study Support

Volunteered to assist junior students struggling with Electrical Machines and Circuit Theory concepts during exam preparation periods. Created simplified concept notes and conducted informal doubt-clearing sessions, which reinforced my own understanding while contributing to a supportive academic community.

⏱️ Deadline Coordination – Semester Project Sprint

Managed the internal timeline for a multi-component semester project, maintaining a shared task tracker and scheduling daily check-ins during the final week. Ensured all members submitted their portions 12 hours before the deadline, leaving buffer time for integration and testing.

Global Awareness

Ethics & Sustainability

Engineering decisions shape societies. I am committed to practicing technology responsibly.

🌱

Sustainability First

Every engineering solution must weigh its environmental cost. I advocate for designs that minimise carbon footprint, reduce material waste, and support the transition to a circular energy economy.

☀️

Renewable Energy Transition

India's energy future depends on harnessing solar, wind, and green hydrogen potential. As an electrical engineer, I see my role in accelerating this transition through intelligent grid design and storage innovation.

🤖

Ethical AI in Engineering

AI must be deployed transparently, without bias, and with human oversight. In my projects, I prioritise interpretability and question whether models make decisions that are fair, safe, and accountable.

⚖️

Responsible Engineering

Engineers hold public safety in their hands. I believe in adhering to codes of practice, prioritising safety over shortcuts, and acknowledging limitations honestly rather than overpromising outcomes.

🌍

Energy Accessibility

Over 750 million people worldwide still lack reliable electricity. Technology must not widen inequality — it must bridge it. I aspire to contribute to affordable, decentralised energy solutions for underserved communities.

🔒

Data Privacy & Security

In connected systems — smart meters, IoT grids — protecting user data is non-negotiable. I practice secure coding principles and consider privacy implications at every design stage.

"As future engineers, we are not just problem-solvers — we are custodians of the planet and society. Every circuit we design, every algorithm we build, and every system we deploy carries consequences far beyond the lab. I choose to engineer with conscience, curiosity, and care."

Vision

Future Goals & Roadmap

A clear path from where I am today to the engineer I aim to become.

202601

Secure Core / Tech Placement

Appear in campus placements targeting core electrical roles (power sector, EV companies, automation firms) and tech-adjacent roles in data analytics and embedded systems. Prepare through mock interviews, aptitude practice, and technical project showcasing.

202602

Master Advanced AI & ML Tools

Complete structured learning in TensorFlow, PyTorch, and deep learning for engineering applications. Apply these skills to real-world EV and power system datasets — deepening the interdisciplinary expertise that sets modern engineers apart.

202703

Explore M.Tech / Higher Studies

Evaluate M.Tech options in Power Electronics, Energy Systems, or Robotics at premier Indian institutes (IITs/NITs) or international universities. Concurrently assess MBA pathways to blend technical and strategic business acumen.

202804

Become an Industry-Ready Engineer

Gain 2+ years of professional experience in a high-impact role — contributing to smart grid deployment, EV battery management, or AI-driven industrial systems. Build a portfolio of impactful, real-world projects.

∞05

Lifelong Learning Strategy

Commit to continuous upskilling through NPTEL, Coursera, industry conferences, and peer learning networks. Stay current with breakthroughs in energy storage, quantum computing, and sustainable engineering — because the best engineers never stop learning.

Self Assessment

Conclusion & SWOT Analysis

Honest self-reflection is the foundation of genuine growth.

💪 Strengths

  • Strong analytical and mathematical foundation in EE core subjects
  • Interdisciplinary approach — combining AI, IoT, and power engineering
  • Practical hands-on experience through projects and industrial training
  • Consistent work ethic and disciplined self-study habits
  • Effective communicator and collaborative team player

🔧 Weaknesses

  • Limited exposure to large-scale industrial software (ETAP, PSS/E)
  • Working to improve confidence in spontaneous public presentations
  • Need to broaden experience with hardware prototyping and PCB design
  • Can be overly self-critical, slowing decision-making at times

🚀 Opportunities

  • India's massive EV adoption push — growing demand for EV engineers
  • Renewable energy sector expansion (solar, green hydrogen)
  • AI integration in power grids creating new hybrid role demand
  • Research collaborations and PDEU's industry-academia network
  • International exposure through global engineering programs

⚠️ Threats

  • Highly competitive job market for core EE roles post-graduation
  • Rapid technology shifts requiring constant skill updates
  • AI automation potentially displacing routine engineering tasks
  • Information overload — difficult to focus learning amid rapid tech change

This SWOT analysis is not a judgement — it is a compass. My strengths give me confidence; my weaknesses give me direction. The opportunities ahead excite me, and the threats remind me to stay sharp, stay curious, and never become complacent. I am proud of the engineer I am becoming, and deeply committed to the engineer I am still working to be. The journey is the destination.

Testimonials

What colleagues, mentors, and educators say about working with Bhumika.

Bhumika is among the most intellectually curious students I have mentored at PDEU. Her project on fault detection in induction motors demonstrated a rare ability to connect theoretical electrical engineering concepts with modern machine learning techniques. She asks the right questions, prepares thoroughly, and communicates her findings with admirable clarity. I have no doubt she will make a meaningful impact in the field.
